user_query:
Read input/notes.md and input/requirements.yaml, then package our new internal skill called "data-ingest-pro" into a complete, well-structured documentation and onboarding bundle. Use the guidance in the files to decide on the best structure and resources. Deliver the following under output/:

1) output/SKILL.md — A cleaned, publication-ready skill doc for data-ingest-pro. Requirements:
- Start with a YAML front matter block including at least: version, name, description.
- Include an "## Overview" section that concisely explains what the skill enables and when to use it (ground it in the provided notes).
- Use a capabilities-based structure with a "## Core Capabilities" section containing at least four distinct capabilities, each as its own subheading (e.g., "### 1. ...").
- Add a short workflow subsection to show the typical ingest→validate→transform→export sequence.
- Include a "## Resources" section that lists and briefly describes scripts/, references/, and assets/ directories (and refer to the specific files you create below).
- Remove any boilerplate/template scaffolding language (do not include phrases like "Structuring This Skill" or TODO markers).

2) output/scripts/validators.py — A Python stub that fits the skill’s resource organization and demonstrates entry points for data validation. Requirements:
- Implement function stubs: validate_csv(...), validate_json(...), validate_xml(...), each with a docstring.
- Include a main guard that prints "validators ready".

3) output/references/guide.md — A concise reference/guidelines doc aligned to the skill. Requirements:
- Include sections for "Schema", "Naming", and "Async Task Orchestration".
- In the "Async Task Orchestration" section, clearly explain how to handle priority, retry/backoff, dependencies, concurrency, timeout, and logging in a generic, tool-agnostic way.

4) output/assets/templates/README.md — Briefly describe what kinds of mapping/transform templates would live here and how users should apply them.

5) output/onboarding/slides.md — A Markdown slide deck for internal onboarding with '---' as slide separators (compatible with Marp/Slidev/Reveal.js). Requirements:
- Include at least 8 slides: Title, Problem, Solution, Architecture, Workflow, Capabilities, Rollout Plan, FAQ/Q&A (must include a slide that clearly says "Q&A").
- The title slide should mention "data-ingest-pro" by name and include a short tagline.

6) output/landing/landing.md — An SEO-oriented landing page draft for the skill. Requirements:
- Include a line starting with "Meta Title: " (50–60 characters) and a line starting with "Meta Description: " (150–160 characters).
- Include an H1 that contains the phrase "data ingestion".
- Provide a structured body with H2/H3 hierarchy, a concluding CTA, and an FAQ section with at least 3 Q/A pairs (use "Q:" and "A:" labels).
- Include a line with "SEO Score: NN/100" followed by a brief breakdown list of at least 5 factors (e.g., keyword usage, content length, readability, headings, meta tags, internal links, external links, FAQ), written as plain text.

Be consistent with naming (data-ingest-pro) across all artifacts, and base content decisions on the notes and requirements. If any details are ambiguous, make sensible assumptions and document them briefly in the relevant file.
